items.title: Sztuczna inteligencja w ubezpieczeniach: czy aktuariusze są gotowi na AI? Perspektywa Aon

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this KPMG ON AIR podcast features Ka Hei Choi (Aon) in conversation with Tomasz Dąbkowski (KPMG) about how artificial intelligence is changing actuarial work. Topics covered include AI maturity and organisational readiness, responsibility and regulatory pressure, the need for transparency and governance, and infrastructure considerations such as GPUs and future‑proof architectures.
bullets: How technology has transformed actuarial practice over recent decades.
Assessment of AI maturity and readiness of people and organisations.
AI as a high‑potential but high‑risk tool (described as a 'Ferrari').
Regulatory pressure, human accountability, and the importance of transparency and governance.
Infrastructure focus on GPUs and architectures resilient to future needs.
takeaways: Prepare infrastructure (notably GPU capacity and scalable architectures) to support increased AI-driven modelling workloads.
Anticipate greater demand for transparent governance, auditability, and human accountability in AI outputs, which will affect compliance and operations.
Expect AI to change productivity and workload patterns in modelling teams—plan capacity, SLAs, and skill development accordingly.
Regulatory pressure in insurance implies tighter requirements for data handling, model explainability and operational controls that impact procurement and vendor selection.

items.title: PoWa™ CRB 1910 Jogging with the FlexPendant

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this video demonstrates how to jog PoWa™ the CRB 1910 cobot using the FlexPendant and is intended to get users up and running quickly. It also directs viewers to ABB Robotics' free online e-learnings and to local teacher-led training options for further instruction.
bullets: Shows jogging the PoWa™ CRB 1910 cobot via the FlexPendant.
Aimed at quick startup and operator familiarization.
References ABB Robotics free online e-learnings and tutorials.
Offers contact route for teacher-led training at local ABB Training Centers.
Geared toward technicians and operators needing hands-on control guidance.
takeaways: Quick jog tutorial supports faster operator onboarding and initial commissioning.
Free online e-learnings enable remote upskilling ahead of on-site work.
Teacher-led training provides a pathway for hands-on validation and deeper competence.
Official ABB resources give consistent, manufacturer-provided guidance for correct use.
